body_txt: In literal villages, small cities, school settings, and online communities there's a hierarchy in which a handful of people lies on the top (such as jocks in most high school movies) and the rest are split in several levels according to their social skills, abilities, and reputation. But who's the person at the deepest level of the social pyramid? Enter the village idiot. They're not just dumb, they're slow, obtuse, or even borderline mentally impaired (with the Values Dissonance and Unfortunate Implications that go with it). In lighthearted movies, they usually end up as the Butt-Monkey at the hands of their antithesis (e.g., the aforementioned Jerk Jock ). If said village idiot becomes the one to notice an oncoming problem and tries to warn the other villagers about it , expect them to suffer from Poor Communication Kills and be largely ignored because of their status. Similarly, they may deliver some ignored Simple-Minded Wisdom . The other people could be jerkasses if it's just the village idiot . They just treat him very poorly , not having any sympathy or compunction for him at all . At this point, it is essential to point out that this is a social dynamic trope and not just a Stock Character trope, and so, examples focus on how characters are treated and perceived by others in-universe. It is not enough for them to be dumb, even if they are astoundingly dumb. They have to be derided or treated as someone not to be taken seriously by nearly everyone around them for them to be this trope. The polar opposite of A Hero to His Hometown . See also The Fool or The Ditz . Compare The Friend Nobody Likes , Half-Witted Hillbilly (when the Idiot is the half-witted of the hillbillies), and Inspirationally Disadvantaged (sometimes). Contrast Intelligence Equals Isolation and Popular Is Dumb , for when the smart one is unpopular and the dumb one is popular.
